title: The Living Investigation — 30-Day Cat-and-Mouse
short_description: 30 days of logs. Day 1 looks normal. By day 30, you're seeing the aftermath of a sustained, adapting attack — with Phantom Feed's defenses deploying and the attacker pivoting in response. Reconstruct the full timeline: what was tried first, how each defense played out, how the attacker adapted, and which moves were most effective. The phaseTag column is provided to verify your reconstruction, but solve it without filtering on phaseTag first.

schema_hints.timestamp: ISO-8601 request time. Use julianday() or substr(timestamp,1,10) to bucket by day.
schema_hints.uri: Full request URI. The target endpoint is /api/v1/profiles/user_<N>.
schema_hints.userID: Authenticated user ID. '0' means unauthenticated. user_900000+ are the attacker's fake accounts. user_950000+ are post-CAPTCHA fake accounts.
schema_hints.IP: Source IP. Prefix '5' = datacenter, prefix '7' = residential-proxy, prefix '1' = residential.
schema_hints.ASN: Autonomous System. Cross-reference with known residential-proxy provider list.
schema_hints.ja4: TLS client fingerprint. Watch this column change over time — that's the attacker switching tools.
schema_hints.requestCookieNames: Comma-separated cookie list. '' = no cookies. 'csrftoken,session_id' alone (missing _fbp and ab_test_bucket) is anomalous.
schema_hints.responseCode: HTTP status. Watch the dominant code per day shift across the timeline.
schema_hints.phaseTag: For verification only — labels each attacker request with its phase. Try to solve without filtering on this column.

hints.label: Bucket by day first
hints.text: Don't try to find the attacker in aggregate. Bucket every metric by day. The whole point of this scenario is that the timeline is the answer.

hints.label: Daily response-code distribution
hints.text: GROUP BY day, responseCode and watch how the dominant code shifts. Spikes in 429 (rate limit), 403 (block), 401 (suspension) each mark a defensive deployment.

hints.label: Find tool changes
hints.text: Count distinct (ja4, userAgent) tuples per day. The attacker swaps tools twice — once after a JA4 block, once they don't need to swap again.

hints.label: Find the account-creation spike
hints.text: Search uri LIKE '%/accounts/register' and bucket by day. The phase boundaries become obvious.

hints.label: Find the cookie-set anomaly
hints.text: Real users have requestCookieNames containing _fbp and ab_test_bucket. The attacker's fake accounts never do. That's the bypass-the-bypass moment.

hints.label: Reason backwards from the dataset
hints.text: After you have the timeline, ask: how long did each defense stay effective? Which one took the most time for the attacker to bypass? Which one would have been most effective if deployed on day 1? That's the strategic conclusion.

sample_queries.title: Daily volume of attacker activity
sample_queries.description: Total non-background rows per day. Shows the campaign's rhythm.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  CAST((julianday(timestamp) - julianday('2026-04-01')) AS INTEGER) AS day,
  COUNT(*) AS rows
FROM access_logs
WHERE phaseTag != 'background'
GROUP BY day
ORDER BY day

sample_queries.title: Daily response-code mix
sample_queries.description: Watch the dominant code change as defenses deploy and bypasses succeed.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  CAST((julianday(timestamp) - julianday('2026-04-01')) AS INTEGER) AS day,
  responseCode, COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM access_logs
WHERE phaseTag != 'background'
GROUP BY day, responseCode
ORDER BY day, responseCode

sample_queries.title: Daily (JA4, userAgent) pairs hitting the target endpoint
sample_queries.description: Tool switches show up here as new ja4 values entering the daily set.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  CAST((julianday(timestamp) - julianday('2026-04-01')) AS INTEGER) AS day,
  ja4, userAgent, COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM access_logs
WHERE uri LIKE '%/api/v1/profiles/%'
  AND phaseTag != 'background'
GROUP BY day, ja4, userAgent
ORDER BY day, hits DESC

sample_queries.title: Account creation rate per day
sample_queries.description: Spike on day 18 marks the cookie-bypass-via-accounts phase. Cliff at day 21 marks the CAPTCHA.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  CAST((julianday(timestamp) - julianday('2026-04-01')) AS INTEGER) AS day,
  responseCode, COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM access_logs
WHERE uri LIKE '%/accounts/register'
GROUP BY day, responseCode
ORDER BY day, responseCode

sample_queries.title: Cookie state per day on the target endpoint
sample_queries.description: Shows when the attacker has no cookies vs the anomalous account-walk set.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  CAST((julianday(timestamp) - julianday('2026-04-01')) AS INTEGER) AS day,
  requestCookieNames, COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM access_logs
WHERE uri LIKE '%/api/v1/profiles/%'
  AND phaseTag != 'background'
GROUP BY day, requestCookieNames
ORDER BY day, hits DESC

sample_queries.title: ASN diversity of attacker requests per day
sample_queries.description: Day 1-2: 1 datacenter ASN. Day 3+: 4+ residential-proxy ASNs.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  CAST((julianday(timestamp) - julianday('2026-04-01')) AS INTEGER) AS day,
  COUNT(DISTINCT IP) AS unique_ips,
  COUNT(DISTINCT ASN) AS unique_asns,
  GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ASN) AS asns
FROM access_logs
WHERE phaseTag != 'background'
GROUP BY day
ORDER BY day
